text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, small caps led, tech and semis surged. Indices rose with IWM +0.93% and SPY +0.39% as semis and software outperformed on AI spending headlines from Nvidia and Arm Holdings and upbeat earnings from Zoom and Take-Two Interactive. Positioning stayed cautious with SPY PCR_vol 1.26 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.57 even as Fear & Greed printed 62.6, suggesting dip-buying rather than chase behavior. Cross-asset confirmed benign conditions with TLT +0.55%, UUP +0.14%, and VIX 16.7 below its 20-day 17.6, keeping the tape orderly.

INDICES — VIX 16.7 fell versus its 20-day 17.6. Index returns: IWM +0.93%, DIA +0.60%, QQQ +0.42%, SPY +0.39%, with GLD -0.76% and SLV -1.57%. STYLE showed SMALL > LARGE by 0.51pp with small +0.93% and large +0.42%. The most notable cross-asset move was UNG -3.44% versus USO -1.14% and TLT +0.55%, signaling duration demand and commodity softness while SPY remained above its 200-day, avoiding a CTA sell trigger.

VOLUME — SPY volume ran roughly inline with the 0.90x index average while QQQ traded lighter at 0.8x, signaling low-conviction tech strength rather than aggressive accumulation. DIA 1.1x and RSP 1.1x showed elevated volume with price up, indicating accumulation in cyclicals and equal-weight. Sector-wise, XLE 1.1x and XLK 0.9x drew attention while XLP 0.7x and XLB 0.7x were ignored, reinforcing the pro-cyclical, growth-tilt narrative.

SECTORS — XLV Healthcare +1.17%, XLK Technology +1.00%, XLU Utilities +0.78%, XLI Industrials +0.73%, XLE Energy +0.61%, XLB Materials +0.54%, XLF Financials +0.41%, XLY Discretionary +0.40%, XLP Staples +0.17%, XLRE Real Estate +0.13%, XLC Comms -0.55%. Industry standouts were CIBR +2.59% and ROBO +2.42% versus laggards BOAT -2.55% and KWEB -2.61%.

THE 7s — Top baskets were Semi +3.04%, Software +2.41%, Health +1.99%, Buildout +1.95%, Power +1.50%, with Semi strength led by INTU +4.19% in Software and likely NVDA strength in Semi while weaker names underperformed less visibly. Bottom baskets were Housing -0.35%, Insurance -0.42%, Old Metals -0.21%, Mag -0.05%, Energy +0.14%, with Mag dragged by underperformers inside a flat basket while relative strength names offset losses, showing internal divergence.

FED & LIQUIDITY — FFR target stayed 3.50–3.75% with next FOMC on Jun 17 and CME probabilities at hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0% while the stance tally showed 6 hawks, 5 neutrals, and 4 doves. USD liquidity stress scored 37/100 (Normal) with Reserve Scarcity at the 77th percentile as the most-stressed component and SOFR vs T-Bill at the 1st percentile, while TED spread sat a benign 0.09% and ON-RRP printed $1.0B.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.26 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.57 with positive skew (SPY +4.0%, QQQ +6.2%) signaled heavy protection demand and put premium despite index gains. SPY GEX_flip at 750.0 and QQQ GEX_flip at 625.0 sat below current prices, implying dealers remain long gamma and dampen intraday swings. VIX 16.7 and VXN 22.8 both below their 20-day averages alongside MOVE 78.4 above its 74.6 average showed modest bond-vol stress but equity-vol complacency.

CROSS-ASSET — Gold GLD -0.76% and silver SLV -1.57% fell with UUP +0.14% and TLT +0.55%, showing dollar strength and real-yield worries weighing on metals despite an equity bid. Oil USO -1.14% and UNG -3.44% weakness alongside XLE +0.61% and Power basket +1.50% underscored equity investors favoring energy equities over commodities on AI power-buildout narratives rather than spot pricing.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 62.6 signaled Greed with selective caution. Top individual movers included INTU +4.19% (Software 7), IJS +0.87% on 10.9x volume (S&P Style ETFs), and EWH +1.53% on 2.9x volume (Countries). On 20d momentum, best sector was XLK +12.59% and worst sector XLB -3.14%, while best basket was Semi +24.57% and worst basket Old Metals -10.63%. Vol-spike names like INTU +4.19% on 3.4x volume suggested accumulation, while EWP 0.00% on 5.9x volume hinted at distribution or rotation without price confirmation.

WATCH — INTU +4.19% deserves attention given 3.4x volume, Software 7 leadership, and strong AI-adjacent software momentum. IJS +0.87% with 10.9x volume and SIZE factor BULL zscore +0.14 signals institutionally-driven small-cap interest that could extend if IWM +0.93% holds. GLD -0.76% with ATM_IV 15.9%, PCR_vol 0.76, and skew +1.9% warrants watching as low put demand and falling price may precede a sharper de-risking if UUP +0.14% and MOVE 78.4 keep pressing higher.
